name: Ibi - Serra del Menejador Trail (PR-CV 26)
name_original: Parque Natural de la Font Roja — Circular al Menejador (PR-CV 26)
description: Hike the difficult 9.0-mile Ibi - Serra del Menejador Trail (PR-CV 26) in Parc Natural del Carrascal de la Font Roja, gaining 2134 feet.
region_intro: Hiking trails around Ibi are characterized by mountainous terrain, diverse natural landscapes, and well-established ecological routes. Situated at 816 meters above sea level, Ibi is surrounded by imposing mountains and gorges, including the Menejador and Biscoi ranges. The region features significant natural highlights such as the Parc Natural del Carrascal de la Font Roja, offering elevated hiking experiences and immersion in local flora and fauna. Best hiking trails around Ibi The most popular hiking route is Ibi - Serra del Menejador Trail (PR-CV 26), a difficult 9.0 miles (14.5 km) trail that takes 5 hours 5 minutes to complete. This route offers substantial elevation gain within the Parc Natural del Carrascal de la Font Roja. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ice Makers' Trail (PR-CV 127), a moderate 9.6 miles (15.5 km) path. This trail leads through varied terrain, showcasing historical elements and natural beauty. Local hikers also love the Menejador Summit – Elephant's Trunk Spring loop from Ibi, a 10.1 miles (16.3 km) trail leading through mountainous landscapes and notable natural features, often completed in about 5 hours 34 minutes. Hiking around Ibi is defined by mountainous terrain, natural parks like Carrascal de la Font Roja, and rugged gorges. The network of trails offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to more demanding treks. The routes in Ibi are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 600 hikers have used komoot to explore Ibi's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Ibi?
A: Hiking around Ibi is characterized by its mountainous terrain, with the town situated at 816 meters above sea level. You'll find diverse natural landscapes, including rugged gorges and the significant &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ibi,_Spain" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parc Natural del Carrascal de la Font Roja&lt;/a&gt;. The region is at the foothills of the Menejador and Biscoi mountain ranges, offering varied elevations and scenic views.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners in Ibi?
A: Yes, Ibi offers a range of trails for various fitness levels. Among the 53 available routes, 13 are classified as easy. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33812614" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Geodetic Survey Monument loop from Ibi&lt;/a&gt;, which is a shorter, less strenuous option.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking in Ibi?
A: Ibi's trails lead through areas rich in natural beauty and historical elements. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ibi,_Spain" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parc Natural del Carrascal de la Font Roja&lt;/a&gt;, known for its flora and fauna. Notable features include the "Elephant's Trunk Spring" (Trompa del Elefante), which is part of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14661337" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Menejador Summit – Elephant's Trunk Spring loop from Ibi&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter historical structures like those along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e868686944/ice-makers-trail-pr-cv-127" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ice Makers' Trail (PR-CV 127)&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any notable viewpoints along the trails in Ibi?
A: Yes, the mountainous surroundings of Ibi offer several panoramic viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2604644" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carrasqueta Pass&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, climbs to 1020 meters and provides beautiful panoramic views towards the interior of Alicante province, and even of Alicante city and the sea. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3081180" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Buitreres and Alt de les Pedreres viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; also offers stunning vistas.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking trails in Ibi?
A: While specific family-friendly routes are not detailed, Ibi has developed ecological routes and local footpaths (SL) that cater to various fitness levels, including easier walks. Look for trails marked as 'easy' in the komoot guide, which are generally more suitable for families.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Ibi?
A: Many natural areas in Spain, including those around Ibi, are generally dog-friendly, especially on marked trails. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in natural parks like the &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ibi,_Spain" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parc Natural del Carrascal de la Font Roja&lt;/a&gt;, to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Check local regulations for specific restrictions.

Q: Are there many circular hiking routes around Ibi?
A: Yes, Ibi offers several circular hiking routes. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14661337" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Menejador Summit – Elephant's Trunk Spring loop from Ibi&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 10.1-mile trail. Circular routes are convenient for returning to your starting point without needing additional transport.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Ibi?
A: Given Ibi's elevation and mountainous terrain, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with milder temperatures. Summers can be hot, especially at lower elevations, while winters can be cooler. Always check the weather forecast before heading out.

Q: Is parking available at trailheads in Ibi?
A: Yes, many trailheads around Ibi, especially those for popular routes, typically have designated parking areas. As local authorities have developed 'adequate ecological routes,' accessibility, including parking, is often considered. It's advisable to check specific route details on komoot for parking information.

Q: How can I reach the hiking trails around Ibi using public transport?
A: Ibi is a town with public transport connections. While direct public transport to every trailhead might be limited, you can often reach the town center by bus and then access nearby trails on foot. For more remote trailheads, a short taxi ride from Ibi might be necessary. Planning your route on komoot can help identify starting points accessible from the town.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Ibi?
A: The hiking routes in Ibi are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 90 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse mountainous terrain, the immersion in local flora and fauna within natural parks like &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ibi,_Spain" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carrascal de la Font Roja&lt;/a&gt;, and the well-established network of trails that cater to various ability levels.

Q: Are there more challenging hikes available in Ibi?
A: Yes, Ibi offers several challenging hikes for experienced trekkers. Out of the 53 routes, 5 are classified as difficult. A prime example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e868667014/ibi-serra-del-menejador-trail-pr-cv-26" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibi - Serra del Menejador Trail (PR-CV 26)&lt;/a&gt;, which is 9.0 miles long with substantial elevation gain within the Parc Natural del Carrascal de la Font Roja.
tips: With its summit at 1350 meters, Menejador offers incredible views in all directions. To reach its highest point, you have to pass a few meters from the forest box and follow a path through the trees until it ends. Returning by an alternative path you will have other views, all of them amazing.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this hike?
faqs.answer: The Ibi - Serra del Menejador Trail (PR-CV 26) is a challenging hike that typically takes around 5 hours and 5 minutes to complete. This duration can vary based on your pace, stops for rest, and time spent enjoying the viewpoints.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as difficult due to its significant elevation gain of over 650 meters and some steep, rocky sections. It's best suited for experienced hikers with good physical fitness.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route typically begins in Ibi, near Les Hortes Park. An information panel for PR-CV 26 is located there. Parking is generally available in the vicinity of Les Hortes Park, but it's advisable to arrive early, especially during peak seasons, to secure a spot.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on the hike?
faqs.answer: You'll experience diverse Mediterranean forest landscapes. The trail includes areas with pine forests, ancient yew trees, and sections with calcareous rock. Expect varying paths, from rocky, stony areas (canchales) in steep ascents, to forest tracks, and narrower trails winding through the slopes.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Ibi - Serra del Menejador Trail?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the summit of Alto del Menejador (1,356 m) with its panoramic views, historical "pozos neveros" (snow wells) like Cava Coloma, and old farmhouses such as Mas de Foiaderes. You can also spot traces of human activity like charcoal production sites and a characteristic rock arch near the summit.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate-high difficulty, significant elevation gain, and challenging terrain, this trail is generally not recommended for beginners or young children. It requires a good level of physical fitness and hiking experience.

faqs.question: Is the Ibi - Serra del Menejador Trail (PR-CV 26) dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on this trail. However, as it passes through the Carrascar de la Font Roja Natural Park, it is crucial to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety and comfort of other hikers. Always carry waste bags and clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the Carrascar de la Font Roja Natural Park?
faqs.answer: There are typically no specific permits required or entrance fees to hike the PR-CV 26 trail within the Carrascar de la Font Roja Natural Park. However, visitors are expected to respect park regulations, such as staying on marked paths, not disturbing wildlife, and practicing Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this trail is during the spring (April-May) and autumn (September-November) when temperatures are milder and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summers can be very hot, and winters may bring cold temperatures and occasional snow at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Ibi - Serra del Menejador Trail (PR-CV 26) covers significant sections of other official trails, including "Ibi - Serra del Menejador" (89% of the route) and "Vuelta al la hoya por el Menejador" (49%). It also briefly intersects with parts of "Camí dels Geladors" and "Camí de l'Horta del Pont".

faqs.question: Are there any unique natural features or wildlife to look out for?
faqs.answer: The trail is part of the Carrascar de la Font Roja Natural Park, known for its rich ecological environment. You can observe diverse flora, including ancient yew trees, Holm oaks, and Valencian oak. While wildlife sightings can be elusive, the park is home to various bird species and small mammals.
